frameworks.name: inductive bias
frameworks.one_line: domain-general priors that constrain learning and generalization from sparse input

frameworks.name: causal perception
frameworks.one_line: events are perceived as causally contingent rather than merely correlated

core_question: What domain-general inductive biases do people hold, and how pervasive are they across perception and reasoning?
key_assumptions: human learners bring structured priors to perception, not blank-slate statistics
some inductive biases are domain-general rather than tied to specific content
manipulations: review-style examination of candidate domain-general biases (e.g., causality)
appeal to Michotte-style sparse launching-event displays as evidence
key_findings: causality is identified as a candidate pervasive, domain-general inductive bias
the impression of causation arises from extremely sparse visual information
interpretations: observers infer causal structure without rich prior knowledge of the environment
such pervasive biases plausibly shape generalization across domains
